Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and complete the Flask backend application in main.py. Specifically, ensure all routes for the eight pages are correctly implemented as per the requirements document, including reading and writing data from local text files in the 'data' directory. Address any logical or functional issues in the existing code. Coordinate with the frontend developer to ensure backend data and endpoints align with the frontend templates and UI elements.
Composition.Programmer 2: Develop all required HTML template files for the 'EventPlanning' web application inside the 'templates' directory. This includes creating 'dashboard.html' and the other seven page templates (events.html, event_details.html, book_tickets.html, participants.html, venues.html, venue_details.html, schedules.html, bookings.html). Each template must implement the page design and elements as specified in the requirements document, including correct element IDs, page titles, and UI components. Coordinate with the backend developer to ensure templates correctly consume the data passed from Flask routes.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of the following elements on each page
